attribution: Curated starter fiqh pack with cited references. This is not a fatwa service.
disclaimer: This pack presents sourced school-based views for common topics. It does not replace qualified scholarly advice for context-heavy questions.

topics.title: Five Daily Prayers
topics.summary: The five daily prayers are obligatory for eligible adult Muslims across the schools represented in the app.
topics.scholarEscalation: If illness, travel, menstruation, post-partum bleeding, safety, or work constraints affect prayer timing or form, ask a qualified local scholar for case-specific guidance.

rulings.summary: Perform the five daily prayers within their times.
rulings.notes: The basic obligation is agreed upon. Details differ for missed prayers, shortening or combining, and who is currently excused.
rulings.contextChanges: Travel, illness, danger, incapacity, menstruation, and post-partum bleeding change how or whether the duty is performed at that moment.

evidence.title: Prayer is prescribed for the believers at fixed times.

topics.title: Ramadan Fasting
topics.summary: Fasting Ramadan is obligatory on eligible adult Muslims, with recognized exemptions and make-up rules.
topics.scholarEscalation: If pregnancy, breastfeeding, chronic illness, travel, work hardship, or medical treatment affect fasting, ask a qualified scholar and doctor for personalized guidance.

rulings.summary: Fast Ramadan from true dawn to sunset when you are eligible and not presently excused.
rulings.notes: The core obligation is agreed upon. What counts as a valid excuse, fidyah, and make-up timing can differ in application.
rulings.contextChanges: Illness, travel, menstruation, post-partum bleeding, pregnancy, breastfeeding, and medical risk can change the ruling and the type of compensation owed.

evidence.title: Fasting is prescribed for you as it was prescribed for those before you.
Whoever witnesses the month should fast it, while the ill and traveler have make-up days.

topics.title: Zakat al-Mal
topics.summary: Zakat on qualifying wealth is obligatory, but eligibility details vary by school and financial context.
topics.scholarEscalation: Because nisab, debts, retirement accounts, business assets, and local tax structures can change the result, ask a qualified scholar when your finances are not straightforward.

rulings.summary: Pay zakat when your qualifying wealth reaches nisab and the required holding period or condition is met in your school.
rulings.notes: The underlying obligation is agreed upon. The treatment of debts, receivables, pension funds, jewelry, and business inventory differs across schools.
rulings.contextChanges: Eligibility can change with asset type, debt load, ownership timing, and whether the wealth is accessible or productive.

evidence.title: Take charity from their wealth to purify them.

topics.title: Witr Prayer
topics.summary: The schools agree Witr should be maintained, but they differ on whether it is obligatory, wajib, or a strongly emphasized sunnah.
topics.scholarEscalation: If you regularly miss Witr, are making up missed prayers, or need to combine a late schedule with tahajjud and night prayer, ask a qualified scholar for a practical routine.

rulings.summary: Witr is wajib after Isha and should not be treated as optional in normal circumstances.
rulings.notes: Hanafi fiqh distinguishes Witr from the five fard prayers, while still treating it as a binding duty that should be made up if missed.
rulings.contextChanges: Travel, exhaustion, and missed Isha affect timing, but not the baseline duty in the school.

evidence.title: Reports used by jurists regarding the consistent maintenance of Witr.
How Important Is Witr in the Hanafi School?

rulings.summary: Witr is a highly emphasized sunnah and among the most important regular voluntary prayers, but it is not treated as obligatory by the majority.
rulings.notes: These schools strongly encourage consistent observance and may describe Witr as among the confirmed sunnahs.
rulings.contextChanges: Sleep plans, travel, and whether you expect to wake later in the night can change when you pray it, not the baseline recommendation.

evidence.citation: Sahih Muslim, Book of Travelers' Prayer
evidence.title: Reports describing Witr as distinct from the prescribed obligatory prayers.
Majority view on Witr as a confirmed sunnah rather than an obligation

rulings.summary: Witr is part of Salat al-Layl and is strongly recommended, but not obligatory.
rulings.notes: Ja'fari fiqh treats Witr within the broader night prayer structure rather than as a standalone wajib prayer.
rulings.contextChanges: Its timing and performance depend on whether the person is offering the full night prayer routine or only the Witr portion.

evidence.title: Rules on the supererogatory prayers, including Salat al-Layl and Witr

topics.title: Shellfish and Non-Fish Sea Animals
topics.summary: Schools differ on shellfish and other non-fish sea animals. This is a classic area where users should compare sources and follow their school or scholar.
topics.checklistLabel: Review seafood guidance for your school
topics.scholarEscalation: If you are switching schools, following a local mosque, or dealing with mixed ingredients and restaurant uncertainty, ask a qualified scholar for guidance tied to your practice.

rulings.summary: Fish are permitted, while shellfish and most non-fish sea animals are generally not permitted; shrimp or prawns are treated separately by some later Hanafi discussions.
rulings.notes: This topic bundles several creatures together, so a simple yes or no answer can be misleading. Fish are the clear baseline permissibility in the school.
rulings.contextChanges: Whether a creature counts as fish, and how shrimp or prawns are classified, changes the result.

evidence.title: Lawful to you is the game of the sea and its food.
The sea's water is pure and its dead are lawful.
Is Seafood Halal in the Hanafi School?

rulings.summary: Sea animals, including shellfish, are generally treated as permissible in the majority schools represented here.
rulings.notes: Individual edge cases still appear in detailed manuals, but the broad rule is much wider than the Hanafi and Ja'fari positions.
rulings.contextChanges: Preparation method, cross-contamination, and whether a product is actually marine can still matter.

evidence.title: Lawful to you is the game of the sea and its food.
The sea's water is pure and its dead are lawful.
What Seafood Is Permissible? The Four Schools

rulings.summary: Fish with scales are permitted and shrimp is permitted, while shellfish and other non-scale sea animals are generally not permitted.
rulings.notes: Because the topic mixes fish, shrimp, and shellfish together, users should read the narrower rule rather than assuming all seafood is handled the same way.
rulings.contextChanges: Whether the animal is a scaled fish, shrimp, or another marine creature changes the result.

evidence.title: Are Muslims allowed to eat shrimp? Other sea animals?
